root_text.sn12.46:1.2: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn12.46:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.46:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, so karoti so paṭisaṁvedayatī”ti?
root_text.sn12.46:2.2: “‘So karoti so paṭisaṁvedayatī’ti kho, brāhmaṇa, ayameko anto”.
root_text.sn12.46:3.1: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, añño karoti, añño paṭisaṁvedayatī”ti?
root_text.sn12.46:3.2: “‘Añño karoti, añño paṭisaṁvedayatī’ti kho, brāhmaṇa, ayaṁ dutiyo anto.
root_text.sn12.46:3.3: Ete te, brāhmaṇa, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.46:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.46:4.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn12.46:1.2: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn12.46:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn12.46:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, are he who does the deed and he who experiences the result one and the same?”
translation_text.sn12.46:2.2: “‘He who does the deed and he who experiences the result are one and the same’: this is one extreme, brahmin.”
translation_text.sn12.46:3.1: “Then is he who does the deed one and he who experiences the result another?”
translation_text.sn12.46:3.2: “‘He who does the deed is one and he who experiences the result is another’: this is the second extreme.
translation_text.sn12.46:3.3: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.46:3.5: Choices are a requirement for consciousness. …
translation_text.sn12.46:3.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.46:3.7: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, choices cease.
translation_text.sn12.46:3.9: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.’”
translation_text.sn12.46:4.1: When he said this, the brahmin said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn12.46:4.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
